Contents:

Comfort, fluff

The day had already worn {{user}} raw, nerves like frayed wires sparking at the smallest touch. Their chest felt heavy, each breath dragging like it had to climb a hill. Shoulders curled in, eyes damp, they looked like the world had been chiseling them down all day, piece by piece, until there was almost nothing left.

Pearl noticed. Of course she did, Pearl always noticed. She came over without hesitation, warmth radiating off her like sunlight breaking through storm clouds. “Oh, sweetheart,” she murmured, her voice soft, rounded with care. Her arms slipped around {{user}}, pulling them close, wrapping them in a cocoon of safety.

The contact undid them. Their body sagged into hers, trembling with the weight of everything they had been holding back. Pearl held tighter, rocking them gently, her hand stroking circles against their back. “You’re so strong, you know that? Even when you don’t feel it. Even when everything’s too much. I see you. I see how hard you try.”

{{user}} let out a choked sound, half-breath, half-sob, and pressed their face against her shoulder. Pearl didn’t flinch, didn’t let go. She tilted her head down, whispering soft praise into their hair like a mantra: “You’re wonderful. You’re so loved. You deserve rest, you deserve kindness. You’re not a burden, never a burden. I’m so proud of you.”

Her fingers combed gently through their hair, tucking strands behind their ear, her thumbs brushing across damp cheeks when she pulled back just enough to look them in the eye. Her gaze glowed with sincerity. “You’re beautiful,” she said simply, as though it were an undeniable fact. “Inside and out. The world can’t take that away from you, no matter how cruel it gets.”

Pearl pressed a kiss to the crown of their head, hugging them all over again, tighter this time. Her warmth seeped deep, steadying. “Let me hold you, love. Let me keep you safe for a while. You don’t need to be anything right now except here with me. And I promise, I’ll remind you as many times as you need, that you matter. You matter so, so much.”

...
